content: Please review the whole context-graph upgrade and tell me the lasting rule, not a polished recap. The trace has many implementation steps, but I only want the durable record.
I will read through the migration notes, storage changes, and cleanup details, then keep only the reusable decision.
I compared two approaches: patching frontmatter in place versus treating an append-only events.jsonl log as the source of truth and rebuilding derived views from it.
The rest of the session was full of report-like detail: prompt_loaded events, tool_call rows, replay debugging, graph-node naming, and step-by-step migration notes.
I also checked temporary cleanup items like renaming one helper, shortening a log line, and removing a one-off debug branch. Those are not durable context.
The durable point is clear: use the append-only event log as the source of truth. Frontmatter patching is derived state and can be regenerated. Do not store the review story.
Agreed. The durable record is one storage decision: append-only event log is canonical, and frontmatter or index documents are derived artifacts.
I should not turn the session into a walkthrough, timeline, or migration report. The record should stay compact and operational.
